---
id: "DM_R042"
title: "DRV Sequential Numbering Rule"
type: "rule"
category: "data_management"
date_created: "2025-01-28"
date_modified: "2025-01-28"
author: "Claude"
status: "active"
version: "1.0"
weight: 42
implements:
  - "MP064": "ETL-Derivation Separation Principle"
related_to:
  - "DM_R028": "ETL Data Type Separation Rule"
  - "DM_R041": "ETL Directory Structure Rule"
  - "DM_R037": "Company-Specific ETL Naming Rule"
---

# DM_R042: DRV Sequential Numbering Rule

## Rule Statement

**All DRV (Derivation) files MUST use sequential numbering that matches their internal P-number codes**: The external filename must directly correspond to the internal processing code to ensure clear execution order and prevent sequencing errors.

## Naming Format

### Required Pattern

```
{platform}_D{group}_{seq}.R
```

**Components**:
- `{platform}`: Three-letter platform_id (cbz, amz, eby)
- `D`: Fixed literal indicating Derivation
- `{group}`: Two-digit group number (01, 02, etc.)
- `{seq}`: Two-digit sequence number indicating execution order (03, 04, 05, etc.)

### Examples

```yaml
valid_patterns:
  - "cbz_D01_03.R"  # Platform CBZ, Group 01, Sequence 03
  - "amz_D02_15.R"  # Platform AMZ, Group 02, Sequence 15
  - "eby_D01_07.R"  # Platform EBY, Group 01, Sequence 07
  - "cbz_D01_04.R"  # Platform CBZ, Group 01, Sequence 04
  - "all_D01_99.R"  # Cross-platform, Group 01, Sequence 99

invalid_patterns:
  - "cbz_DRV01_customer.R"     # ❌ Old pattern with descriptive suffix
  - "cbz_D1_3.R"               # ❌ Missing zero-padding
  - "cbz_D01_customer.R"        # ❌ Non-numeric sequence
  - "cbz_DRV_03.R"             # ❌ Using DRV instead of D
  - "cbz_D01.R"                # ❌ Missing sequence number
```

## Internal P-Number Mapping

### Required Comment Structure

Each DRV file MUST contain its P-number identifier on line 2:

```r
#####
#P07_D01_03
# DRV: Customer DNA Analysis (Group 01, Sequence 03)
```

### Filename-to-P-Number Correspondence

The external filename MUST match the internal P-number:

```yaml
mapping_requirements:
  internal_code: "#P07_D01_03"
  external_file: "cbz_D01_03.R"

  validation:
    - P07 -> Platform identifier (maps to cbz/amz/eby)
    - D01 -> Group 01
    - 03  -> Sequence 03

  result: "cbz_D01_03.R"
```

## Execution Order Rules

### Sequential Processing

DRV files are executed in strict numerical order within each group:

```yaml
execution_sequence:
  group_01:
    - "cbz_D01_03.R"  # Executes first
    - "cbz_D01_04.R"  # Executes second
    - "cbz_D01_05.R"  # Executes third
    - "cbz_D01_07.R"  # Executes fourth (gap allowed)

  group_02:
    - "cbz_D02_01.R"  # New group starts at any number
    - "cbz_D02_05.R"  # Continues sequentially
```

### Dependency Management

```r
# In orchestration scripts
execute_drv_group <- function(platform, group) {
  # Find all DRV files for this platform and group
  pattern <- sprintf("%s_D%02d_[0-9]{2}\\.R", platform, group)
  drv_files <- list.files("DRV", pattern = pattern, full.names = TRUE)

  # Sort by sequence number (already in filename)
  drv_files <- sort(drv_files)

  # Execute in order
  for (drv_file in drv_files) {
    message(sprintf("Executing: %s", basename(df_file)))
    source(df_file)
  }
}
```

## Benefits

### Clear Execution Order
- Sequence is immediately visible from filename
- No need to open files to determine order
- Prevents out-of-sequence execution errors

### Simplified Orchestration
- Natural sorting works correctly
- Easy to identify missing sequences
- Group-based parallelization possible

### Direct Code Mapping
- External name matches internal identifier
- Reduces confusion and mapping errors
- Simplifies debugging and maintenance

### Version Control Benefits
- Clear diff visibility for sequence changes
- Easy to track execution order changes
- Simplified merge conflict resolution

## Migration from Old Pattern

### Step 1: Identify Old Pattern Files

```r
# Find files using old DRV naming pattern
old_pattern_files <- list.files(
  "DRV",
  pattern = ".*_DRV[0-9]+_.*\\.R$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)

# Example old patterns to migrate:
# cbz_DRV01_customer.R -> cbz_D01_03.R
# cbz_DRV02_sales.R    -> cbz_D01_04.R
# cbz_DRV03_product.R  -> cbz_D01_05.R
```

### Step 2: Extract P-Numbers

```r
# Read P-number from each file
extract_p_number <- function(file_path) {
  lines <- readLines(file_path, n = 10)
  p_number_line <- grep("^#P[0-9]+_D[0-9]+_[0-9]+", lines, value = TRUE)

  if (length(p_number_line) > 0) {
    # Extract P-number components
    p_number <- sub("^#", "", p_number_line[1])
    return(p_number)
  }
  return(NULL)
}
```

### Step 3: Generate New Names

```r
# Convert P-number to new filename
p_number_to_filename <- function(p_number, platform) {
  # Parse P-number: P07_D01_03
  parts <- strsplit(p_number, "_")[[1]]

  if (length(parts) == 3) {
    group <- sub("D", "", parts[2])
    seq <- parts[3]

    new_name <- sprintf("%s_D%s_%s.R", platform, group, seq)
    return(new_name)
  }
  return(NULL)
}
```

### Step 4: Rename Files

```r
# Rename file to new pattern
migrate_drv_file <- function(old_path) {
  # Extract P-number
  p_number <- extract_p_number(old_path)

  if (!is.null(p_number)) {
    # Determine platform from old filename
    filename <- basename(old_path)
    platform <- substr(filename, 1, 3)

    # Generate new name
    new_name <- p_number_to_filename(p_number, platform)

    if (!is.null(new_name)) {
      new_path <- file.path(dirname(old_path), new_name)

      # Rename file
      file.rename(old_path, new_path)
      message(sprintf("Migrated: %s -> %s",
                     basename(old_path), new_name))
      return(TRUE)
    }
  }

  warning(sprintf("Could not migrate: %s (missing P-number)",
                 basename(old_path)))
  return(FALSE)
}
```

## Validation Functions

### Validate Filename Format

```r
validate_drv_filename <- function(filename) {
  # Check pattern: {platform}_D{group}_{seq}.R
  pattern <- "^([a-z]{3})_D([0-9]{2})_([0-9]{2})\\.R$"

  if (!grepl(pattern, filename)) {
    stop(sprintf("VIOLATION DM_R042: Invalid DRV filename format: %s",
                filename))
  }

  # Extract components
  matches <- regmatches(filename, regexec(pattern, filename))[[1]]

  return(list(
    platform = matches[2],
    group = matches[3],
    sequence = matches[4]
  ))
}
```

### Validate P-Number Correspondence

```r
validate_p_number_match <- function(file_path) {
  filename <- basename(file_path)

  # Get expected components from filename
  components <- validate_drv_filename(filename)

  # Read P-number from file
  p_number <- extract_p_number(file_path)

  if (is.null(p_number)) {
    stop(sprintf("VIOLATION DM_R042: No P-number found in file: %s",
                filename))
  }

  # Check if P-number matches filename
  expected_suffix <- sprintf("D%s_%s",
                            components$group,
                            components$sequence)

  if (!grepl(expected_suffix, p_number)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "VIOLATION DM_R042: P-number mismatch in %s\n  Expected: *_D%s_%s\n  Found: %s",
      filename, components$group, components$sequence, p_number
    ))
  }

  message(sprintf("✅ DM_R042: Valid DRV file: %s matches %s",
                 filename, p_number))
  return(TRUE)
}
```

### Validate Execution Order

```r
validate_drv_sequence <- function(df_directory) {
  # Get all DRV files
  drv_files <- list.files(drv_directory,
                         pattern = ".*_D[0-9]{2}_[0-9]{2}\\.R$",
                         recursive = TRUE)

  # Group by platform and group
  file_df <- data.frame(
    file = drv_files,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Parse filenames
  file_df <- file_df %>%
    mutate(
      platform = substr(file, 1, 3),
      group = substr(file, 5, 6),
      sequence = as.integer(substr(file, 8, 9))
    ) %>%
    arrange(platform, group, sequence)

  # Check for duplicate sequences
  duplicates <- file_df %>%
    group_by(platform, group, sequence) %>%
    filter(n() > 1)

  if (nrow(duplicates) > 0) {
    stop(sprintf("VIOLATION DM_R042: Duplicate sequences found:\n%s",
                paste(duplicates$file, collapse = "\n")))
  }

  # Report sequence gaps (informational)
  gaps <- file_df %>%
    group_by(platform, group) %>%
    summarize(
      sequences = list(sequence),
      gaps = list(setdiff(
        seq(min(sequence), max(sequence)),
        sequence
      ))
    ) %>%
    filter(lengths(gaps) > 0)

  if (nrow(gaps) > 0) {
    message("INFO: Sequence gaps found (this is allowed):")
    print(gaps)
  }

  message(sprintf("✅ DM_R042: All %d DRV files have valid sequences",
                 nrow(file_df)))
  return(TRUE)
}
```

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] All DRV files follow `{platform}_D{group}_{seq}.R` pattern
- [ ] No descriptive suffixes in filenames
- [ ] All files contain matching P-number on line 2
- [ ] Sequence numbers are zero-padded (01, 02, not 1, 2)
- [ ] Group numbers are zero-padded
- [ ] Platform IDs are exactly 3 lowercase letters
- [ ] Files are in DRV/{platform}/ directory structure
- [ ] Orchestration scripts use sequential execution
- [ ] Old pattern files have been migrated
- [ ] Validation functions pass for all files

## Relationship to Other Rules

### Supersedes
- Old DRV naming pattern in DM_R028 (`{platform}_DRV{nn}_{purpose}.R`)

### Complements
- **MP064**: Maintains ETL-Derivation separation
- **DM_R041**: Follows directory structure requirements
- **DM_R028**: Aligns with overall ETL/DRV organization

## Conclusion

The DRV Sequential Numbering Rule (DM_R042) establishes a clear, unambiguous naming system for derivation files that directly maps external filenames to internal processing codes. This eliminates execution order confusion, simplifies orchestration, and ensures maintainable data processing pipelines.
